revision.comment: 1 revision imported: importing programmatically generated pages
Adding [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]

revision.text: [[Category:pages to be overwritten]][[en:Module:BaseConvert/doc]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects in Module]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text: [[Category:pages to be overwritten]][[en:Module:BaseConvert/doc]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects in Module]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace Module]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:BaseConvert/doc]][[Category:qq]]

revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Bot: remove deleted category Wikipedia overwrites
Added the page to [[:Category:Module documentation pages]]
revision.text: {{High-use}} Converts numbers to a specified base between 2 and 36, for use in templates such as {{Tl|Binary}}, {{Tl|Octal}}, {{Tl|Hexadecimal}}, etc. ==Usage== local BaseConvert = require('Module:BaseConvert') BaseConvert.convert({n = 14600926, base = 16}) -- returns 'DECADE' Arguments: *'''n''' - (required) the number to be converted, as a string. It may be a number instead, if the input base is 10. *'''base''' - (required) the base to which the number should be converted. May be between 2 and 36, inclusive. *'''from''' - the base of the input. Defaults to 10 (or 16 if the input has a leading '0x'). Note that bases other than 10 are not supported if the input has a fractional part. *'''precision''' - number of digits to be rendered after the radix point. Trailing zeros will be added if needed. If not specified, however many digits are needed will be shown, up to 10. *'''width''' - minimum number of digits to be rendered before the radix point. Leading zeros will be added if needed. *'''default''' - Value to return if n is empty or non-numeric. Defaults to the value of n. *'''prefix''' / '''suffix''' - wikitext to add before/after the returned result. Will not be added if n is empty or non-numeric. For example, you might use a prefix of 0x when converting to hex, or a suffix of 8 when converting to octal. ===From templates=== In wikimarkup, this module may be called with a function name ''n''to''m'', e.g.: {{Markup |{{#invoke:BaseConvert|16to10| FF }}|{{#invoke:BaseConvert|16to10| FF }} |{{#invoke:BaseConvert|10to36|500}}|{{#invoke:BaseConvert|10to36|500}} |{{#invoke:BaseConvert|10to16|Foo|default=0}}|{{#invoke:BaseConvert|10to16|Foo|default=0}}}} All options above are supported, excluding {{Para|base}}, {{Para|from}} and {{Para|n}} which are set by the mandatory options. ===Edge cases=== {{Markup |{{#invoke:BaseConvert|10to10|500}}|{{#invoke:BaseConvert|10to10|500}} |{{#invoke:BaseConvert|10to10|FooBar}}|{{#invoke:BaseConvert|10to10|FooBar}} |{{#invoke:BaseConvert|10to10|FooBar|default=}}|{{#invoke:BaseConvert|10to10|FooBar|default=}} |{{#invoke:BaseConvert|10to16|Foo}}|{{#invoke:BaseConvert|10to16|Foo}}}} {{Math templates}} [[Category:Wikipedia utility modules]] [[pt:Módulo:BaseConvert/doc]] [[Category:Module documentation pages]]

{{High-use}} Converts numbers to a specified base between 2 and 36, for use in templates such as {{Tl|Binary}}, {{Tl|Octal}}, {{Tl|Hexadecimal}}, etc. ==Usage== local BaseConvert = require('Module:BaseConvert') BaseConvert.convert({n = 14600926, base = 16}) -- returns 'DECADE' Arguments: *'''n''' - (required) the number to be converted, as a string. It may be a number instead, if the input base is 10. *'''base''' - (required) the base to which the number should be converted. May be between 2 and 36, inclusive. *'''from''' - the base of the input. Defaults to 10 (or 16 if the input has a leading '0x'). Note that bases other than 10 are not supported if the input has a fractional part. *'''precision''' - number of digits to be rendered after the radix point. Trailing zeros will be added if needed. If not specified, however many digits are needed will be shown, up to 10. *'''width''' - minimum number of digits to be rendered before the radix point. Leading zeros will be added if needed. *'''default''' - Value to return if n is empty or non-numeric. Defaults to the value of n. *'''prefix''' / '''suffix''' - wikitext to add before/after the returned result. Will not be added if n is empty or non-numeric. For example, you might use a prefix of 0x when converting to hex, or a suffix of 8 when converting to octal. ===From templates=== In wikimarkup, this module may be called with a function name ''n''to''m'', e.g.: {{Markup |{{#invoke:BaseConvert|16to10| FF }}|{{#invoke:BaseConvert|16to10| FF }} |{{#invoke:BaseConvert|10to36|500}}|{{#invoke:BaseConvert|10to36|500}} |{{#invoke:BaseConvert|10to16|Foo|default=0}}|{{#invoke:BaseConvert|10to16|Foo|default=0}}}} All options above are supported, excluding {{Para|base}}, {{Para|from}} and {{Para|n}} which are set by the mandatory options. ===Edge cases=== {{Markup |{{#invoke:BaseConvert|10to10|500}}|{{#invoke:BaseConvert|10to10|500}} |{{#invoke:BaseConvert|10to10|FooBar}}|{{#invoke:BaseConvert|10to10|FooBar}} |{{#invoke:BaseConvert|10to10|FooBar|default=}}|{{#invoke:BaseConvert|10to10|FooBar|default=}} |{{#invoke:BaseConvert|10to16|Foo}}|{{#invoke:BaseConvert|10to16|Foo}}}} {{Math templates}} [[Category:Wikipedia utility modules]] [[pt:Módulo:BaseConvert/doc]] [[Category:Module documentation pages]]
